============================= test session starts ==============================
platform linux -- Python 3.11.6, pytest-8.1.1, pluggy-1.4.0
rootdir: /home/ci/checkout-service
collected 14 items

tests/test_cart.py ......                                                 [ 42%]
tests/test_pricing.py ...F..                                              [ 85%]
tests/test_orders.py ..                                                   [100%]

=================================== FAILURES ===================================
_________________ test_discount_never_exceeds_subtotal _________________________

    def test_discount_never_exceeds_subtotal():
        cart = Cart(items=[Item("sku-88", 12.50)])
        pricing = Pricing(rules=load_rules("rules/promo.yaml"))
>       assert pricing.apply(cart).discount <= cart.subtotal
E       AssertionError: computed discount 15.00 exceeds cart subtotal 12.50
E        +  where 15.00 = Pricing.apply(...).discount

tests/test_pricing.py:47: AssertionError
=========================== short test summary info ============================
FAILED tests/test_pricing.py::test_discount_never_exceeds_subtotal
================== 1 failed, 13 passed in 0.42s =================================
query: which test failed and what was the assertion where the discount exceeds the cart subtotal?
gold_answer: AssertionError: computed discount 15.00 exceeds cart subtotal 12.50
notes: pytest run with 13 passing lines; the assertion-detail line carries the failure answer and uniquely shares 'discount/exceeds/subtotal' with the query, so bm25 keeps it at a tight budget while forced_only (path-only), recency, and llmlingua_style drop it. The file:line traceback path (audit-critical for fault localization) is on a separate line and is force-kept.
